title: lobelia nummularioides var. prostrata
intro: Lobelia nummularioides var. prostrata, also known as creeping lobelia, is a low-growing, prostrate annual plant native to North America, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers and leaves. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of lobeline and other alkaloids. The health benefits of creeping lobelia include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Creeping lobelia is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
